title: TEST A — honest-verification hook (variant A: the 90% trap)
body:
🎓 The "90% win rate" trap — and how to fact-check any signal channel in 2 minutes

Almost every signal channel flexes a giant win rate in its bio. Here's the uncomfortable truth about that number — and how to check it yourself before you trust anyone with your money.

Three ways that win rate gets inflated:

1️⃣ The TP1 trick. Most "signals" list 3–4 TP exit targets. Price only has to tap the first, smallest one for the call to be screenshotted as a "win" — even if it later reverses into the stop. The calls that go straight to SL? Quietly deleted.

2️⃣ Screenshots prove nothing. A cropped green chart has no date, no full history, no losing trade beside it. Anyone can show you their best day. Nobody weak shows you their worst week.

3️⃣ No public, loss-inclusive log. If you can't see EVERY call — the red ones too, time-stamped, in order — the headline number is marketing, not a track record.

How to actually verify someone (2 minutes):
✅ Ask for the full history, losses included. A real one has them — and won't flinch at the question.
✅ Go on-chain. On exchanges like Hyperliquid, every trader's positions and fills are public on the blockchain — you can pull anyone's real, uncut P&L. No cherry-picking possible. We use on-chain data to check traders before we trust them — and so can you.

Our take: we'd rather show you a real record with red days in it than a shiny number you can't verify. Honesty is the one edge that compounds.

Save this. Next time a channel waves a win rate at you, you'll know exactly what to ask. 👉 {{COMMUNITY}}

#CryptoSchool
